copy.title: Tigers edge Rays in ten. Brewers demolish Giants. Knicks-Spurs Finals start tomorrow night.
sportsOther: Tigers walked out of Tampa with a one-run escape they had no business winning.
Brewers didn't just beat the Giants—they obliterated them by fourteen.
Rockies snatched one in Angel Stadium that the Angels will replay all night.

markets.mood: Tech got split down the middle today — Nvidia and Apple climbed while Microsoft tanked 3.4%, leaving the broad market basically flat and looking for direction.
markets.whyBullet1: Your S&P 500 index fund didn't move, but Microsoft's drop is a real warning that big bets on AI infrastructure are getting re-priced in real time.
markets.whyBullet2: Watch Thursday and Friday for earnings whispers and whether the Fed signals anything about rate cuts — that's what'll actually move the needle past the noise.
markets.bringUp: Bitcoin dropped 4.6% while stocks stayed flat, which means the correlation between crypto and tech risk is still alive and the smart money is hedging against something.

culture.topic: Pokémon TCG turns 30, card game explodes
culture.whatHappened: Pokémon Company launched the 30th Celebration for their trading card game, cashing in on nostalgia and collector demand.
culture.whyItMatters: The TCG market is ridiculous right now—graded cards from the 90s sell for thousands, and this drops new product directly into that fever.
culture.whatToSay: Dude, remember when you could buy a booster pack for three bucks? Yeah, those cards are worth real money now.

culture.topic: Jimmy Kimmel just roasted Trump at Peabodys
culture.whatHappened: Kimmel won a Peabody Award and thanked Trump by name 'Pumpkin McPornhumper' during his acceptance speech.
culture.whyItMatters: Late-night comedy is merging with actual award recognition, and Kimmel's still the guy willing to say what network people won't.
culture.whatToSay: Did you see what Kimmel said at the Peabodys? Absolute madman move.

culture.whatHappened: Adrien Brody as a Hungarian-Jewish architect who arrives in America after WWII and tries to build something real.

copy.finalSharpTake: The Tigers barely held off Tampa in a sloppy one, Milwaukee demolished San Francisco, and Colorado walked off the Angels. Baseball's been messy all week, which means nobody's figured out anything yet. Tomorrow the Knicks and Spurs tip off for real stakes, and that's where the actual tension lives right now. Markets flatlined, tech stocks went nowhere, and Bitcoin got hammered again, so everyone's just waiting for something to break.
